# Use the following style guide in the current design task

## Name of the styleguide: `mobile-01-brutalistluxury_light`

Use the above name in `spawn_agent` if you want to pass it to subagents.

# Brutalist Luxury Mobile Dashboard — Style Guide

---

## Style Summary

### Description

A deep black canvas mobile interface that fuses raw brutalist typography with refined luxury accents. Bebas Neue condensed display sans-serif delivers bold, architectural headlines while Cormorant Garamond italic provides editorial contrast. Matte gold (#C9A962) serves as the singular accent against a near-black foundation (#0D0D0D), creating dramatic emphasis through restraint. Sharp-edged rectangles, zero corner radii, and no shadows establish brutalist geometry, while generous letter-spacing and vertical rhythm create breathing room within the darkness. Suited for premium lifestyle apps, high-end fitness trackers, and luxury products targeting users who appreciate bold minimalism and editorial sophistication.

### Key Aesthetics

- **Brutalist Architecture:** Raw, unadorned forms with sharp geometric precision—zero corner radii and no shadows throughout.
- **Editorial Dual-Typeface:** Bebas Neue for all headlines, labels, and navigation; Cormorant Garamond italic for descriptions and body text.
- **Monochromatic Drama:** Near-black palette with multiple black values (#0D0D0D, #1A1A1A, #2A2A2A) creating subtle depth.
- **Singular Gold Accent:** Matte gold (#C9A962) used sparingly for active states, CTAs, and emphasis—no other accent colors.
- **Typographic Monumentalism:** All-caps Bebas Neue headlines with wide letter-spacing (+6px on section labels) as architectural elements.
- **Square Indicators:** Brutalist rectangles replace soft circles for status and checkboxes. Vertical bar charts with sharp edges.

### Tags

`mobile` · `dark-mode` · `brutalist` · `luxury` · `editorial` · `gold-accent` · `high-contrast` · `minimal` · `sophisticated` · `sharp`

---

## Color System

Color creates hierarchy through dramatic restraint. Gold is the only accent color. Multiple black values create subtle depth without distraction. Text colors descend in warmth from cream white to cool grays. No semantic color variations—gold serves all purposes.

### Core Backgrounds

- #0D0D0D — Page Background (deep black)
- #1A1A1A — Card Surface, Input Surface
- #2A2A2A — Muted Surface (subtle elevation)

### Text Colors

- #F5F4F2 — Text Primary (warm cream white)
- #8A8A8A — Text Secondary (descriptions, labels)
- #7A7A7A — Text Tertiary (placeholders)
- #6A6A6A — Text Muted (inactive items)
- #5A5A5A — Text Disabled (lowest contrast)

### Border Colors

- #2A2A2A — Border Subtle; card strokes, dividers
- #1A1A1A — Border Strong; tab bar top stroke
- #5A5A5A — Divider; content separators within cards

### Accent Colors

- #C9A962 — Gold Primary; active states, CTAs, featured card strokes, section labels
- #0D0D0D — Text On Gold (dark text on gold-filled elements)

---

## Typography

The dual-font system creates deliberate contrast—Bebas Neue handles the commanding, architectural layer (headlines, labels, navigation), Cormorant Garamond handles the refined editorial layer (descriptions, body content). Bebas Neue uses a single weight throughout; Cormorant Garamond alternates between italic body and semibold emphasis.

### Font Families

- **Bebas Neue** — Headlines, section labels, navigation, tab bar labels, metric values, status text
- **Cormorant Garamond** — Descriptions, body text, meta content, accordion titles (italic preferred for body)

### Type Scale

- **48px** — Large Title. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: -1, lineHeight: 0.9
- **42px** — Metric Value. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 1
- **24px** — Schedule Time. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 1
- **22px** — Card Title, Program Name. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 1, lineHeight: 0.95
- **16px** — Headline. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 1, uppercase
- **16px** — Body. Cormorant Garamond, italic
- **16px** — Callout. Cormorant Garamond, semibold 600
- **14px** — Section Label. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 6, uppercase
- **13px** — Footnote. Cormorant Garamond, italic
- **12px** — Caption 1. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 2
- **11px** — Caption 2, Badge. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 3
- **10px** — Tab Bar Label. Bebas Neue, normal, letterSpacing: 2

### Font Weights

- **600** — Semibold. Cormorant Garamond accordion titles, emphasis
- **400** — Normal. All Bebas Neue text, Cormorant Garamond body (italic style)

### Letter Spacing

- **+6px** — Section labels (dramatic tracking)
- **+3px** — Duration badges, small caps
- **+2px** — Status text, captions
- **+1px** — Metric values, card titles, headlines
- **-1px** — Large display titles (tight for massive headlines)
- **0** — Default; body text

### Line Height

- **1.7** — Readable; expanded body content, accordion answers
- **1.2** — Standard; single-line elements
- **0.95** — Compact; multiline program titles
- **0.9** — Tight; large display headlines

---

## Spacing System

### Gap Scale (between elements)

- **32px** — Major; section gaps
- **24px** — Content; wrapper horizontal padding
- **20px** — Section; header to content, schedule card content
- **16px** — Card; cards within rows, program card gaps, habit row internals
- **12px** — Medium; search bar internals, metric card internal
- **8px** — Standard; day column internals, small element groups
- **6px** — Small; tab bar icon to label
- **4px** — Tight; title + description stacks within rows

### Padding Scale

- **[24, 24]** — Content wrapper (horizontal)
- **[24, 24]** — Program cards
- **[20, 20]** — Cards uniform (metric cards, habit rows, schedule content)
- **[14, 16]** — Search bar
- **[14, 0]** — Segment control items (vertical only)
- **[12, 0, 32, 0]** — Tab bar (top, sides, bottom safe area)
- **[10, 16]** — Small buttons

### Layout Pattern

- **402px** — Screen Width (iPhone standard)
- **24px** — Content Padding (horizontal)
- **32px** — Section Gap (vertical, between major sections)
- **16px** — Card Gap (horizontally scrolling items)
- **20px** — Row Gap (stacked cards within section)
- **200px** — Program Card Width (fixed)

---

## Corner Radius

This design system uses **zero corner radius** throughout, reinforcing the brutalist architectural aesthetic. Every element maintains hard, sharp edges. Only iOS-native status bar system elements retain minimal rounding.

- **0px** — ALL elements (cards, buttons, inputs, indicators, progress bars, segments)

---

## Icons

### Icon Style

- **Lucide** — Outlined, normal weight (1.5px stroke)

### Icons Used

- **Navigation:** home, check-square, bar-chart, user (tab bar)
- **Actions:** search, bell, plus, chevron-down, chevron-right

### Icon Sizes

- **22px** — Navigation (tab bar icons)
- **20px** — Notification (header bell)
- **18px** — Search, Chevron/Arrows
- **14px** — Add/Plus (small actions)

### Icon Color States

- #C9A962 — Active (navigation, expanded chevron)
- #5A5A5A — Inactive (tab bar, disabled)
- #8A8A8A — Secondary (collapsed chevron)
- #7A7A7A — Placeholder (search icon)
- #0D0D0D — On Gold (icon on gold background)

Create the following design task in the `bgpjZ` nodes.

  Task:
  Create the dark mode (Nocturne) Export Modal inside node `bgpjZ` in HarmonyForge.pen. This is a 1440x900 frame. The dark modal should mirror the structure of the light mode Export Modal (node LxifV) but using Nocturne dark palette tokens.

Structure to build inside bgpjZ:
1. A dim overlay rectangle (1440x836 at y:64, fill: #2D181726)
2. An ExportModal dialog frame (1200x700, cornerRadius:8, centered at x:120 y:100) containing:
   - LEFT PANE (480w, 700h, fill: $nocturne-bg, vertical layout, right border stroke $nocturne-detail):
     * LeftHeader (fill:$nocturne-bg, h:60, bottom stroke $nocturne-detail): text 'Score Preview' in Instrument Serif 18 fill:$text-on-dark
     * ScorePreview area (fill:#1A1110, height:fill_container, clip:true) with paper-like texture using dark gradient (#2D1817 to #3E2723 at 45deg opacity 0.4), staff lines in $nocturne-detail opacity 0.4, note ellipses in $text-on-dark opacity 0.7, clef symbols 𝄞 𝄢 in $text-on-dark
     * PreviewFooter (h:72, fill:$nocturne-bg, top stroke $nocturne-detail, center layout): IBM Plex Mono 10 '4 voices · 3 measures · 1 violation' fill:$text-on-dark opacity 0.5; Inter 10 'Last edited 2 min ago' fill:$text-on-dark opacity 0.35
   - RIGHT PANE (720w, 700h, fill:$nocturne-detail, vertical layout):
     * RPHeader (h:60, bottom stroke $nocturne-surface/20, space_between, padding [0,32]): TitleGroup with 'Export As' in Instrument Serif 22 fill:$text-on-dark, subtitle 'Choose a format below' Inter 12 opacity:0.45 fill:$text-on-dark; CloseBtn (32x32, cornerRadius:6, transparent fill, stroke $nocturne-detail) with lucide x icon fill:$text-on-dark
     * RPBody (fill_container, vertical layout, gap:20, padding [24,32]): 'FORMAT' label IBM Plex Mono 9 fill:$text-on-dark opacity:0.4; FormatGrid row (3 cards gap:12): PDF card SELECTED (fill:$nocturne-surface, stroke $nocturne-surface 2px) with lucide file-text icon fill:$text-on-dark, 'PDF' IBM Plex Mono 12 500 fill:$text-on-dark, 'Print-ready' Inter 10 fill:$text-on-dark opacity:0.75; MIDI card (fill:$nocturne-bg, stroke $nocturne-detail 1px) with lucide music-2 icon, 'MIDI' label, 'DAW-compatible' sub all fill:$text-on-dark; MusicXML card same style (fill:$nocturne-bg, stroke $nocturne-detail); FormatGrid2 row (4 cards gap:12): PNG, JSON, Audio, ZIP cards all with fill:$nocturne-bg stroke:$nocturne-detail same icon/label pattern with text-on-dark fills
     * RPFooter (h:72, top stroke $nocturne-surface/20, end-justified, padding [0,32]): ExportBtn (h:40, cornerRadius:6, fill:$nocturne-surface, gap:8, padding [0,24]) with lucide download icon fill:$text-on-dark, 'Export PDF' IBM Plex Mono 12 500 fill:$text-on-dark
3. A Header bar at top (1440x64, fill:$nocturne-bg, bottom stroke $nocturne-detail, padding [0,40], space_between): logo icon (lucide music 18x18 fill:$nocturne-surface) + 'HarmonyForge' Instrument Serif 18 fill:$text-on-dark

Remove placeholder when done. Use only design system tokens — no ad-hoc hex values except for the dim overlay (#2D181726) and score preview gradient (#2D1817, #3E2723).
